## Q1 2026 — Brag Doc (Sam Bennett, Engineering Manager, Platform)

Draft — pulled from the contributions log. [Inferred] items flagged for review before sharing.

### People Development

- Took on Nate Brooks (first job out of college) in December 2025; by March 2026 he was leading his first project (Aurora Dashboard) and using structured pushback to protect his timeline from senior scope requests.
- Coached Marcus Walker through an estimation pattern problem; learning-budget process in place by February; by April, Marcus was bringing written fallback designs to 1:1s proactively.
- Supported Sarah Carter in running her first cross-team design review (April 9) — she owned the room.
- Got Laura Hayes, our only data scientist, from "disconnected from engineering roadmap" (January) to leading the Helix Research spike and presenting it at the all-hands (February).

### Decisions and Influence

- Drove the Atlas Migration provider decision (internal OAuth2 platform over external IdP) in March.
- Drove the Atlas token caching decision (Option B: in-memory TTL with LRU) in April 1 1:1 with Sarah.
- Reframed Marcus's Phoenix scope cut as forward motion rather than concession (April 7 blocker review) — important for his morale and the project timeline.
- [Inferred] Facilitated cross-team alignment on the Atlas provider decision across Platform and Payments — Sarah did the work, I helped coordinate the conversation.

### Strategic Alignment

- Delivered portfolio one-pagers to James Miller for all six active Platform projects by April 6.
- Managed the Platform Weekly → Eng Leads Sync information flow cleanly for Q1.
- [Inferred] Helped David Clark spot that Platform is stretched across 6 projects — "over-indexed on Atlas, losing visibility into Phoenix" (his words on March 30).

### Hiring and Team Building

- Participated in the backend senior engineer hiring loop starting March — 3 debriefs so far, one candidate in final round.
- Coordinating Marcus's parental leave coverage plan (12-week leave starting May 18) — pulling Nate in as co-owner.

### Operational Improvements

- Drove the Phoenix parallel-path decision (fallback design + escalation) on April 7.
- [Inferred] Helped push the Sentinel Security project forward in Q1 planning — Chris's proposal was seeded in January and budgeted for Q2.

### Issue Prevention

- Reviewed Sarah's Atlas API spec v2 and caught two correctness issues before they shipped.

⚠ Flagged for review: 4 entries marked [Inferred] above. Verify before sharing with David or James. This draft is about 60% done — Q1 starts on Jan 1, I should pull from January contributions files too.
